title: Mount Ashitaka
summary: Mount Ashitaka (愛鷹山, Ashitakayama) is an eroded extinct  stratovolcano in the area south-east of Mount Fuji, Japan. Its highest peak, 1,504 metres (4,934 ft) high, is Mount Echizen-dake, but the complex is named after its secondary peak, Ashitaka-yama, 1,188 m (3,898 ft) high.
text:
Mount Ashitaka (愛鷹山, Ashitakayama) is an eroded extinct  stratovolcano in the area south-east of Mount Fuji, Japan. Its highest peak, 1,504 metres (4,934 ft) high, is Mount Echizen-dake, but the complex is named after its secondary peak, Ashitaka-yama, 1,188 m (3,898 ft) high.
